summary:
Measuring serum FSH helps identify testicular failure by assessing spermatogenetic capacity; other options are less specific for this distinction. Sentence two omitted as per instruction constraints while maintaining core message relevance. ```plaintext
Measuring serum FSH aids in identifying testicular failure through assessment of spermatogonial activity.
``` Summary:

To determine the source of azoospermia, measure serum FSH to assess spermatogenic function since elevated values indicate testicular failure. Other tests do not provide a clear differentiation between testicular dysfunction and obstructions effectively.
